name: Branching Dialogue
description: A conversation system where player choices, dialogue nodes, conditions, and results move narrative state through authored branches.
design_notes: Branching dialogue needs durable state ownership. Define dialogue_node_id, dialogue_choice_id, branch conditions, result events, localization ids, voice and subtitle synchronization, choice history, and save/load restoration before writing content. The system should make consequences understandable while supporting hidden conditions and delayed outcomes where appropriate.
design_purpose: Let players express intent through dialogue choices that can alter story, quests, relationships, or information.
Give writers a structured way to author conditions, branches, callbacks, and consequences.
Preserve player choice history so later dialogue and world state can react consistently.
player_fantasy: The player feels their words and priorities shape how characters respond and how the story unfolds.

parameters.name: conversation_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the active conversation asset.

parameters.name: dialogue_node_id
parameters.description: Current authored dialogue node in the conversation graph.

parameters.name: dialogue_choice_id
parameters.description: Choice selected by the player and recorded in history.

parameters.name: branch_condition_id
parameters.description: Condition that decides whether a node or choice is available.

parameters.name: choice_timeout_ms
parameters.description: Optional countdown before default or silence choice is selected.

parameters.name: localized_string_id
parameters.description: Localization key for displayed dialogue text.

parameters.name: player_choice_history_id
parameters.typical_range: profile or save scoped history key
parameters.description: Persistent record used by later narrative conditions.

edge_cases: A choice becomes invalid while the conversation is open because quest_stage_id or inventory state changes.
Voice playback, subtitle events, and choice UI must stay synchronized after skip, pause, or language change.
Multiplayer conversations need a policy for which player owns the choice and how others see it.
Save/load during a conversation should restore the node, speaker, camera, and pending consequence safely.
common_bugs: Choice history records display text instead of dialogue_choice_id, breaking localization and save migration.
Branch conditions are checked for display but not rechecked when the player confirms the choice.
Voice line ids and subtitle_event_id records drift after editing dialogue timing.
Skipping a line fires consequence_event_id twice because both skip and natural completion commit the node.
balancing_notes: High-impact choices need enough context that players understand the kind of consequence they are accepting.
Hidden conditions add depth, but too many invisible locked options make dialogue feel arbitrary.
Timed choices create tension but should have defaults and accessibility overrides.
accessibility_notes: Support readable text scaling, subtitle settings, pauseable dialogue, and optional choice timeout extensions.
Do not require players to process voiced dialogue without captions, speaker labels, and conversation logs.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent conversations as graph assets with dialogue_node_id, dialogue_choice_id, localized_string_id, and branch_condition_id fields. Commit choices through a single transaction that records player_choice_history_id, triggers consequence events, and saves the resulting node state.
implementation_notes.godot: Store dialogue graphs as Resources or JSON data and evaluate branch conditions through a narrative-state autoload. UI, subtitles, and voice playback should consume the same node ids so skip and reload behavior remains consistent.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets, data tables, or dialogue plugins with explicit node and choice ids. Server or host authority should own multiplayer choice commits, while UMG reads localized string ids and subtitle events from the selected node.
implementation_notes.web: Keep dialogue graph data in JSON with pure condition evaluators. Commit a choice by writing choice id, branch result id, and world-state flags before rendering the next node, so refresh or reload cannot duplicate consequences.

relationships.reason: Branching dialogue needs choice widgets with focus, localization, timing, and committed choice state.
Dialogue branches can commit reputation changes or faction consequences after player choices.
Cinematic framing can support authored conversations without changing branch logic.

example_games.title: Mass Effect
example_games.note: Uses branching conversations that record choices and affect later story state.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses dialogue choices that can change quest and story outcomes.

example_games.title: Disco Elysium
example_games.note: Uses dialogue branches tied to character state, checks, and consequences.
